critic.verdict: Great rhythmic accuracy and song representation, but features overly complex stream flows and hold coordination for an Easy chart.

issues.problem: Features active holds where the player must perform an 8th-note tap (46.494 tap R, 50.813 tap L) while holding another panel. This coordination is too advanced for an Easy difficulty.
A 6-note 8th-note stream (U-D-R-L-U-D) that forces awkward double-stepping or complex crossovers, which are highly uncomfortable and inappropriate for beginner players.
A 7-note 8th-note stream (D-U-R-L-D-U-R) that has very poor foot flow, requiring heavy double-stepping or double crossovers to execute.

critic.verdict: Playable with decent rhythmic syncopation, but ruined by lazy charting that uses Left-Right jumps exclusively.

issues.problem: Every single jump in the chart is 'JUMP LR' (Left + Right). There is zero jump variety (no UD, LU, LD, RU, or RD jumps), which makes the physical movement feel extremely repetitive and uncreative.

The rapid transition from U -> JUMP LR -> HOLD U -> JUMP LR in eighth notes (0.24s intervals) is highly asymmetric and awkward, forcing the right foot to execute rapid, jerky movements between R and U while the left foot remains relatively static on L.

The dense eighth-note jump pattern (D -> U -> JUMP LR -> U -> D -> JUMP LR) feels very jerky and cluttered due to the constant forcing of the player back into the wide LR stance immediately after vertical steps.

critic.verdict: An energetic chart that matches the song's chaotic humor, but suffers from physically impossible hold patterns and excessive 16th-note jump streams for a Hard rating.

issues.problem: Physically impossible jump. While holding R (from 46.254 to 46.734), the player is forced to jump LD at 46.494. This requires a highly advanced bracket on one side while holding the other, which is inappropriate for a Hard chart.
Unplayable step. The player is holding R (until 50.933) and U (until 51.053) simultaneously, but is prompted to tap D at 50.813, which is physically impossible with only two feet.
Awkward rapid lateral flow. Tapping L (15.544) then R (15.664) as a 16th-note transition is highly uncomfortable and forces a double-step or an intense hip twist.
Overly dense stream. The continuous 16th-note stream from 69.407 to 70.607 containing mid-stream jumps (LR at 69.527 and DR at 70.487) spikes the difficulty too high for this level.
